MINI MINUTES FOR MAR-18-2015 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT HENRY KARNILOWICZ DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION: NON-COMPLIANCE WITH ORDERS OF ABATEMENT ISSUED BY THE SAN FRANCISCO DEPARTMENT OF PUBLIC HEALTH. SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION: PUBLIC NUISANCE. SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION: UNLAWFUL, UNFAIR, AND FRAUDULENT BUSINESS PRACTICES. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (SEE MINI-MINUTES AND/OR ORDER FOR COMPLETE RULING). JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: ANN DELGADO, CSR # 11185; DEPT. 302. =(302/EHG)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-06-2015 Continued To Jun-24-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Defendant Henry Karnilowicz Demurrer To 1st Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Mar-19-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JORDANNA THIGPEN OF ABTAHI THIGPEN LLP, TEL. NO. (213) 863-0100 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendants Henry Karnilowicz, individually and dba Occidental Express's Demurrer To 1st Amended Complaint is Overruled as to the first cause of action: Non-compliance with orders of abatement issued by the San Francisco Department of Public Health. The First Amended Complaint (FAC) is not uncertain, ambiguous or unintelligible. The FAC sufficiently alleges Defendant is targeted by each cause of action (See FAC at 8-9.) The FAC adequately sets forth the ultimate facts necessary to establish each element of the cause of action. (See FAC at 20.) Sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to the second cause of action: Public Nuisance. The FAC does not allege ultimate facts that Defendant violated the San Francisco Electrical Code. (See FAC at 64.) The Court finds there is a split of authority as to the issue of ultimate facts necessary to survive demurrer. (See City of Bakersfield v. Miller, 64 Cal. 2d 93, 97, 410 P.2d 393, 396 (1966); People v. McKale, 25 Cal. 3d 626, 631, 602 P.2d 731, 733 (1979); Sheehan v. San Francisco 49ers, Ltd., 45 Cal. 4th 992, 997, 201 P.3d 472, 476 (2009). Sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to the third cause of action: Unlawful, unfair, and fraudulent business practices. Plaintiff must state with reasonable particularity the facts supporting the statutory elements of the violation. Khoury v. Maly's of California, Inc., 14 Cal. App. 4th 612, 615, 17 Cal. Rptr. 2d 708, 710 (1993). Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Ann Delgado, CSR # 11185 =302/ehg
